LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT MICHAEL MA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BUDEN OF PROOF BASED ON THE DEFENSE OF THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS, CCP 340.5. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PRESENT TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT ON THAT ISSUE. THE EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT THE INJURY OCCURRED ON OR BEFORE JULY 9, 2002, AS A CONSEQUENCE OF PLAINTIFF'S TREATMENT IN JUNE 2002. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM IS BARRED BY THE 3 YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. THE COURT FINDS THE FOLLOWING FACTS PRESENTED BY DEFENDANT TO BE UNDISPUTED, 1;2;3;4 THAT DR. MA SAW PLAINTIFF ON JUNE 12, 2002 AND TREATED PLAINTIFF AS DESCRIBED IN PLAINTIFF'S DISPUTED FACT 4;5 THAT DR. MA TREATED PLAINTIFF ON JUNE 28, 2002 AND "CUT PIECE OF HER GUM'S FLESH TO ELONGATE ANCHOR TEETH #27, #28 AND #32. "AND PLUNGED THREAD AROUND EACH TOOTH TO PUSH GUM AWAY FROM THE TOOTH; 6;7;8;11;12;13;14;16;19. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE PATRICK MAHONEY, REPORTER: NICI RODICH, CSR NO. 11604

"AND PLUNGED THREAD AROUND EACH TOOTH TO PUSH GUM AWAY FROM THE TOOTH; 6;7;8;11;12;13;14;16;19. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.
